chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: the notes are here: https://notes.ietf.org/notes-ietf-126-nmop?edit

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text: Just FYI - The center mic is a little quiet (meetecho)

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @James Cumming for local or remote attendees?

chat.author: James Cumming
chat.text:
Lorenzo Miniero said:

James Cumming for local or remote attendees?

Local

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: I'll let the AV team know

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: A box sent you an update for a path with the exact same attributes as the previously received update, for some reason. And as you were caught in a loop, you received these in mass.

chat.author: Benoît Claise
chat.text: Forgot to mention: Presenters, the allocated time is for presentation and Q/A. If you want some feedback, you know what to do...

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: but why they sent the same attributes in different VPNs, what was the root cause ?

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: They were adding a site for an existing customer, with inter-as option A involved, if I got it right.

chat.author: Christian Dieckhoff
chat.text: Have you considered using the data and ingest it in form of structured data to a symbolic model which would enable for deterministic queries instead of heuristic results?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Thomas, can you share what model you used?  I assert that, yes, _some_ LLMs have _some_ networking knowledge.  How much that is and to what extent vary based on the model.  I find frontier models like Sonnet/Opus _do_ have a fair amount of knowledge up to what I would consider a mid-tier network engineer.  I find some of the open weight models a bit lacking unless you provide more context to them as to how things interact/relate.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Boris, the root cause was that multiple instead of one site-of-origin attributes were used. If the same site-of-origin would have been used, the loop would have been prevented.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Joe, qwem3.5

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Thanks.

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: @Thomas  - thanks, i got it.  But due to misconfig (all VPNs do belong to the same customer)?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: I haven't tried qwen.  I did try gpt-oss-20b.  It did okay with MCP input, but when asked to do more complex network analysis, it did hallucinate elements of BGP operational concepts as well as specific ways to extract BGP info (part of that maybe that I was using Cisco NX-OS rather than more traditional IOS).

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @boris, correct, it was a DCI VPN, connecting multiple data center shared services together

chat.author: Alex Huang Feng
chat.text: a BoF seems like more aligned with the IETF process no?

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: indeed

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: @Thomas - thanks again, really makes me think about a possible defence for such cases...

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: @Alex, yes

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Yeah.  This is starting to feel more like a BoF with the need for chairs and discussion leads.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @joe, since we are feeding the LLM's with YANG semantics yet, it tries to interpret the leaf names and sometimes comes to the wrong conclusions. That was our first take after 6 weeks so far. But we will play, especial in a postmortem/incident setting more on it and feedback at NMOP IETF 127.

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: @Boris one way is to not rewrite AS_Path and let the customer do allowas-in

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: Do you also use skills with your data to "teach" the agent how to interpret YANG data?  I found some value there with a YANG skill.  I also find it useful with my MCP servers to make good use of rich JSON schema to explain the use/meaning of data elements.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @boris, before deploying such configuration changes, we should use a digital twin to perform what-if and prepare verifications. When deploying, verify wherever the config change works according intend or not.

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: @thomas That'd be great. You cannot guess the customer CE behaviour though, if they do funky policy configs

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @Joe, right now we did minimal manual effort to mirror the YANG information to skills. However our intend is to use the YANG schema registry MCP. That will not only make the YANG semantics available but also guide the agent where data needs to be consumed from.

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: @Thomas - yes, interesting approach

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @joe, IETF has chartered a jsonschema working group. I am personally wondering wherever a better alignment between json and YANG schema would help to interpret the data better. Thoughts?

chat.author: Joe Clarke
chat.text: I think perhaps some lower hanging fruit is to make sure we're doing our best to define YANG data nodes with meaningful descriptions that could be mapped into JSON schema parameters.

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: @Joe Exactly. The format won't improve the state of things. The content will.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: +1 to Joe and Pierre

chat.author: Dan Voyer
chat.text: is there not a benchmark document/work for Agent on the way to validate and test these various permutations - and then chose the right approach based on data/facts

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: @Bo one slide mentioned agent capability, there was "a bit" of discussions on that in DAWN this week

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: capability discovery

chat.author: Boris Khasanov
chat.text: IMO, this draft better suits for BMWG

chat.author: Bo Wu
chat.text: @Reshad The capabilities here is refering the network management capabilies  profile, e.g. fault management, network resource verification

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @boris, agree

chat.author: Qin Wu
chat.text: regarding protocol autotest, maybe rasprg might be more relevant and could get interests

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text: @Thomas, question on the mailing list please

chat.author: Pedro Martinez-Julia
chat.text: Why?

chat.author: Pedro Martinez-Julia
chat.text: So?
